assessor_1_raw: Transformer vs logistic regression for readmission prediction. The feature design is intentional - the rich LR baseline tests whether sequence structure adds value beyond severity and recency. Temporal split is appropriate. Preprocessing fit on training only. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: The 36/6/6 month split provides adequate training data. Positional embeddings by sequence order are standard clinical NLP practice. The design acknowledges the single-hospital limitation. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: AUROC as primary metric is appropriate for rare event ranking. Stratified performance checks by disease severity, utilization quartile, and age provide confound detection. Verdict: defense_wins.
